subject: State Farm Claim 46-81Z7-05W — Nataly Danilova — Engineer Report Denial w/ Masterful Counter
adjuster: Nataleigh Cromwell, AIC, AIS, Claim Specialist – Proximity (309-994-9282, statefarmfireclaims@statefarm.com)
forwardedBy: Benjamin Salgado (Roof-ER): 'Ignoring hail damage and says collateral happened before policy period. This is ridiculous'
denialSummary: State Farm sent denial referencing engineer report and combined two separate claims (DOL May 17 vs May 31, 2025). Three layers of denial: (a) roof coverings not functionally damaged by any recent storm, (b) dents on metal components attributable to pre-policy storms, (c) those metal impacts are cosmetic only.
counterText: Dear Ms. Cromwell, I have reviewed your April 7, 2026 letter, along with the March and May photo packets that were provided. I dispute State Farm's denial. Your letter does not provide a clear or reliable basis for denial. It combines two separate claims into a single generalized conclusion, and it also references a reported storm event on May 17, 2025, while the claim letter itself identifies May 31, 2025 as the second date of loss. That inconsistency alone raises concern about the accuracy of the engineer-based analysis you are relying on. In addition, your letter provides only conclusions, not an actual explanation. It does not identify the methodology used by the engineer, the weather data relied upon, the basis for attributing certain impacts to a time prior to September 12, 2024, or the basis for concluding that the roof coverings were not functionally damaged by the reported or any recent storm event. It also does not explain why the contrary findings and photographs I submitted from my contractor were rejected. Your denial appears to rely on multiple theories without clearly reconciling them. As written, State Farm is taking the position that: the roof coverings were not functionally damaged by the reported or any recent storm event; dents on certain metal components are attributable to pre-policy storms; and those metal impacts are cosmetic only. Those are significant conclusions, yet your letter does not explain how State Farm distinguished between old conditions, alleged cosmetic conditions, and any new storm-related damage, especially across two separate claims. Please provide the following: The full engineer report, including all attachments, photographs, diagrams, notes, and any documents reviewed by the engineer. The engineer's name, company, and qualifications. The specific weather data, reports, dates, and sources relied upon. A clear written explanation reconciling the May 17, 2025 date stated in your denial letter with the May 31, 2025 claim date. A claim-specific explanation for each claim number separately, rather than one combined summary. A specific explanation of why my contractor's findings and photographic evidence were rejected. At this time, I do not consider State Farm's April 7 denial to be adequately explained. Please treat this email as a formal dispute of the denial and preserve all claim materials, including the full engineer file, metadata, internal notes, and all documents or data relied upon in reaching your decision. Please respond by email only. Sincerely, Natalia Danilova.
keyDenialLanguage: the roof coverings were not functionally damaged by the reported or any recent storm event
dents on certain metal components are attributable to pre-policy storms
those metal impacts are cosmetic only
carrierTactic: TRIPLE-LAYER DENIAL — combine three theories without reconciling them: (1) no functional damage, (2) damage is pre-policy, (3) damage is only cosmetic. Plus mix multiple claim numbers into one denial letter. Engineer report basis without identifying methodology, engineer credentials, or weather data sources.
rooferTactic: TEMPLATE-QUALITY HOMEOWNER COUNTER — Six specific document requests: (1) full engineer report + attachments, (2) engineer name/company/credentials, (3) weather data + sources, (4) reconcile date inconsistencies, (5) claim-by-claim explanation, (6) why contractor findings rejected. Closes with formal-dispute language + records-preservation demand. This letter should be the canonical template for any State Farm engineer-report denial.
patentMapping: State Farm patents US10565658 (Storm event roof damage detection), US20220414781 (Probable Roof Loss Confidence Scores), US10977734 (real property damage claims analysis) all support this layered-denial pattern. The 'engineer report' is the human cover for AI-generated probability scores below approval threshold.
lessonForAnalyzer: When State Farm cites 'engineer report' with combined theories: counter-letter MUST request (a) engineer credentials, (b) methodology, (c) weather sources, (d) reconciliation of any date inconsistencies between denial letter and claim filing, (e) explanation for rejecting contractor findings. Records preservation demand should be standard.
